0.q: 35S:LcLCYB showed higher total carotenoid content, which was about 3-fold of the CK plants; content of each detected pigment increased significantly in leaves.

1.q: 35S:LcBCH showed higher total carotenoid content, which was about 2.5-fold of the CK plants; content of each detected pigment increased significantly in leaves.

2.q: 35S:LcLCYB transgenic plants showed a higher photosynthetic rate (Pn), maximal photochemical efficiency of PS II (Fv/Fm), and chlorophyll content under NaCl treatments.

3.q: 35S:LcLCYB tobacco seedlings maintained a much lower level of H2O2 and MDA, indicating a stronger ROS scavenging activity of transgenic plants before and after NaCl treatments.

4.q: Their over-expression significantly increased carotenoid accumulation... Up-regulation of almost all the genes involved in the carotenoid biosynthesis pathway.

5.q: Compared to the control plants, total carotenoids in 35S:LcPDS, 35S:LcZDS, and 35S:LcCRTISO increased significantly.

6.q: The content of total carotenoids in 35S:LcCRTISO was significantly higher than those in 35S:LcPDS and 35S:LcZDS.

7.q: LchERF-overexpressing plants showed higher chlorophyll and proline contents, and were associated with lower H2O2 content under salt stress.

8.q: A novel ethylene-responsive transcription factor, named LchERF, was isolated from Lycium chinense. Upon treatment with NaCl, PEG or ethephon (ET), transcript levels of LchERF rapidly increased in L. chinense.

9.q: 35S:LcLCYB showed higher total carotenoid content, which was about 3-fold of the CK plants; content of each detected pigment increased significantly in leaves.

10.q: 35S:LcLCYE showed higher total carotenoid content, which was about 3-fold of the CK plants; content of each detected pigment increased significantly in leaves.

11.q: 35S:LcBCH showed higher total carotenoid content, which was about 2.5-fold of the CK plants; content of each detected pigment increased significantly in leaves.
